name: Newt Gingrich
subject: You’re invited
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee
body:
There aren’t many the President can trust. But you’re one of them. |
---
| | |

Peter,

The coordinated effort to sabotage President Trump by deeply entrenched bureaucrats and an out-of-control news media is something you'd expect from other countries -- _not America._

The time for action is now. We must ensure that President Trump has the support he needs throughout his entire presidency.

Which is why I'm calling your attention to the 2017 President's Trust -- a _reserve_ of funds for targeted ads, true emergencies, and critical grassroots programs.

I'm inviting you, a loyal supporter, to join as a member.
---
| **[Please accept your invitation to become a member of the 2017 President's Trust by Saturday, August 12, 2017 with a generous contribution of $1.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=607c7d00e7ebc6bead497fd98510459c81a1fd2306056fb27173dde5893c45069cfbea4c94a2ec9f0d7009be4fcfca859770ea4a1d79c7d5)**
[CONTRIBUTE $1](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=607c7d00e7ebc6beea231cc6d282b6d4bf49fa86b06d6631a9ebe303e8b778f5d0d99a24afe8a0c5ecce604e7fbc852791db3af483dca264) The President's Trust has been around for past Republican presidents, but I can't think of a time when it was more desperately needed.

It's extraordinary. We're just over six months in to Trump's presidency, but the fix is already in to take him down.

When you have a producer of a major media outlet admit that President Trump is "probably right" to call the phony Russia story a witch-hunt -- but continue to push it 24/7 to boost ratings -- we have a real problem in our country.

What we achieved in November was remarkable. The world watched the American people take their country back. We cannot let what we've achieved collapse.

The time to act is now.

**[Please accept your invitation by Saturday, August 12, 2017 to join the 2017 President's Trust with a generous contribution of $1.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=607c7d00e7ebc6be333389f7a7659dc235453f1659eb029a1b3a96dbaac8d64a0e47dba1d826006c35236a99c60520391d779ecb853a8bf7)**

Thank you,

name: The White House
subject: Meet the Cabinet: Secretary David Shulkin
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec4d096249b1266381d6152f0a24ba61e3d2049ad38c2b504337c8303ee75dd6a46e5048c7335ccda349a90f3793b8edd9

Welcome to 1600 Daily's Meet the Cabinet series, where we showcase members of President Trump's Cabinet.

Meet Secretary David Shulkin

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec2611a2b4bbcb15e2f1866cc16e3af6201710fe7b1369e4b18cc29251f6b4297c72e56461e42130adb83c4cd76a3a73e7

-------------

President Trump and Secretary Shulkin announce Veteran telehealth initiatives designed to provide greater access and care

President Trump and Secretary Shulkin announced three initiatives that will expand access to health care for Veterans across the country. Using telehealth technology and mobile applications, the VA will connect with more Veterans to provide services where they live.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec37fb2ae5bd46d45ffb0d93553ea35bb5816a3c6268506ebafe455e37a41aa46ccd334446b18b7e98637a33937df79f3c
Read more

The VA expands transparency and accountability efforts

Secretary Shulkin announced that the Department of Veterans Affairs is taking a further step on transparency and accountability as a follow-on to the VA Accountability and Whistleblower Protection Act signed by President Trump. The department is making public a list of adverse employee actions taken since January 20. This information is posted at
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec5cd3ac5dc1691ca4e95f42257a07b75a83de22c57fdaa24ce3028f6dcb445006a38b39f876cd785efab71748089f43c2
va.gov/accountability and will be updated weekly.

Secretary Shulkin unveils world’s most advanced commercial prosthesis

Dr. Shulkin, M.D. unveiled the world’s most advanced commercial prosthetic — the Life Under Kinetic Evolution (LUKE) arm — during a visit to the
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ecc084efd23179ccb8a900e487871765c19ae55b0df9bde1cecc1d360a394be2c8db8e494f00ebaef671efb022059cd6e7
VA New York Harbor Health Care System’s Manhattan campus.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec667b76270c401ad9b1f15ece58f23647be24d34b755e371b8f01a2c57662b62e634bdfc819e314cb97940f56c25ae100
Read more

The VA makes wait times for patients transparent for Veterans

Veteran Affairs is taking unprecedented steps to increase transparency. The VA launched a new
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ecffdf3027ed0160aaf8e269ebce6a202a24d2b9e28ab5eae9725a9807ba6df0b48a069e999eb59e0379dd946523767e4b
Access and Quality Tool that provides Veterans with an easy-to-use, easy-to-understand way of accessing patient wait time and quality of care data. This tool not only provides Veterans with more information about VA services, it increases accountability and ensures VA is held to a higher standard.

Photo of the Day

Secretary Shulkin joined President Trump at the White House last week to make a major announcement on Telehealth. (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

Coming Up This Month

This month, Secretary Shulkin will be attending the American Legion National Convention, as well as visiting the VA medical facilities in Nevada and Montana. In September, the Secretary will speak to the National Association of State Directors for Veterans Affairs.

Follow Secretary Shulkin and the Department of Veterans Affairs on

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ecbe09994adea601bd3a452a45baa634c1f27a25f568931cad0a4b8bfddfe344aefd3a6bc8a7fed0e6ef95fd3c71323125
Twitter and
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ecb7bfaeeb591fe8727e9a779026477ab3a854dacce259c6b04a51ad24b3dbc1585c5c568e2709b46cd9ca23ff56bf66c9
Facebook

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ec0a52f9072aa42a67118af4c471a7f6509c0966437448150a3fb7f887ec4a0efd305bbaf50196b460b563ce5f28731d69
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=566ef9048904c2ece25643297824e3f2d09ed95909439e06a41162fb4ebb0149e3b60ffafeb0f7c77a09810e0e15529263a4203b615a12b0
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4f804a80ff657a6a854818c6f943ca5e43b7be4e17736ee11e1ccde2195db172cbf395c75144e5cb0335ce848dc6a4801

subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on UNH Poll Results
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 10, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 ( tel:(202)%20321-5310 )

Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on latest UNH Poll

Concord, NH – NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the
following statement regarding the latest UNH Poll showing
Republican Governor Chris Sununu with a 62% approval rating among
Granite Staters:

"The most recent UNH Poll shows Republican Leadership is working
for New Hampshire. Clearly Granite Staters appreciate Governor
Sununu's bipartisan, optimistic vision and pro-growth policies.
And they agree that under Republican leadership, New Hampshire is
heading in the right direction."

###

clean_body: PRESS RELEASE For Immediate Release: August 10, 2017 Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 ( tel:(202)%20321-5310 ) Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on latest UNH Poll Concord, NH – NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding the latest UNH Poll showing Republican Governor Chris Sununu with a 62% approval rating among Granite Staters: "The most recent UNH Poll shows Republican Leadership is working for New Hampshire. Clearly Granite Staters appreciate Governor Sununu's bipartisan, optimistic vision and pro-growth policies. And they agree that under Republican leadership, New Hampshire is heading in the right direction." Background: Link to latest UNH Poll ( ) footer image

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Preventing ‘fire and fury’ with North Korea
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501130557%2501100261%2b-%2b08-10-2017%2bNorth%2bKorea%2beNewsletter%250114198.6547341.3483292.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501130557%2501100261%2b-%2b08-10-2017%2bNorth%2bKorea%2beNewsletter%250114198.6547341.3483292.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **August 10, 2017** |
---|---|---|---
| | | North Korea poses one of the greatest threats to international security. That's why the response from the Trump Administration is so dangerous. Threatening nuclear war, as the President and some in his circle have done, dramatically increases the likelihood of miscalculation, sows fear and confusion among our partners and allies, and damages our credibility. Hot rhetoric from a U.S. President is exactly what the North Korean leader wants - it elevates his stature on the world stage. We need to work quickly to ramp up pressure on the North Korean regime through tough economic sanctions that drive North Korea to the nuclear negotiating table. The latest round of UN sanctions on North Korea are an important step forward, but it's critical that we distinguish between what's written in a UN resolution and what actually gets enforced. Time and time again we've seen the failure of China and some other nations to enforce these UN sanctions on their banks and businesses. That failure has allowed the North Korean economy to grow and escape the pressure needed to bring them to the negotiating table for meaningful talks. The Congress recently passed one round of sanctions against North Korea. But we must do more to address the loopholes in our current sanctions regime. That's why I've introduced another round of sanctions with Senator Pat Toomey of Pennsylvania. Our bipartisan bill - [the BRINK Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14198.6547341.3483292.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontent%2fvan-hollen-toomey-encourage-adoption-brink-act&redir_log=948371816423614) - is modeled after the successful Iran sanctions laws that brought Iran to the negotiating table to end its nuclear weapons program. It would ratchet up the enforcement of sanctions, turning off the spigot of money being funneled into North Korea from some in the international community, including Chinese banks. It gives international banks and businesses only two options: stop funding Kim Jong Un's dangerous behavior or get cut off from the U.S. financial system. I believe these additional measures, as a part of a broader financial pressure campaign and paired with rigorous diplomacy, are our best chance of peacefully resolving the North Korean nuclear threat. That is why I'm urging Congress to pass additional sanctions - and calling on the President to follow the advice of Teddy Roosevelt: speak softly, and carry a big stick. Chris Van Hollen U.S. Senator
## Finding Cures for Childhood Cancer
No childhood should be interrupted by a struggle for survival, but cancer tragically puts far too many kids in Maryland and across the country in a battle for their lives. That's why earlier this year, I introduced a bipartisan bill to support the development of innovative and promising cancer drugs for children. And I'm pleased to announce that this legislation has passed both the House and Senate and is headed to the President's desk to be signed into law. [The Research to Accelerate Cures and Equity (RACE) for Children Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14198.6547341.3483292.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontent%2fvan-hollen-bill-advance-treatments-childhood-cancer-passes-senate&redir_log=574979544319748) will modernize drug development regulations to help build on the progress being made at institutions like the National Institutes of Health. This is an important step to increase treatment options for kids with cancer. But our job won't be done until we save every child - and their families - from the misery of this horrific disease. ****
## Protecting Funding for Fighting Crime in Baltimore
Cities like Baltimore rely on the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) for a wide range of support to combat violent crime, and I'm working hard on the Senate Appropriations Committee to ensure our state gets the funding it needs on this critical issue. But Attorney General Jeff Sessions recently threatened to withhold funding for Baltimore City police if the city doesn't enforce the Trump Administration's immigration enforcement orders that Maryland's Attorney General, Brian Frosh, says could be unconstitutional. This is a gross abuse of power on the part of Attorney General Sessions, and I've joined with Baltimore's Congressional delegation to [strongly oppose it](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14198.6547341.3483292.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontent%2fvan-hollen-baltimore-congressional-delegation-calls-sessions%25E2%2580%2599-threat-city-funding-fighting&redir_log=890066483621961). Making the threat even more outrageous, it appears that Sessions overlooked the important fact that the Maryland Department of Public Safety and Correctional Services - not the Baltimore City Police Department - technically enforces immigration orders. Therefore, Sessions is demanding that Baltimore Police Chief Kevin Davis and the Baltimore City Police take actions that are outside of their control. Rather than threatening Baltimore and other cities, the Justice Department should be doing what it can to support efforts to reduce violent crime. As we await a response from the DOJ, we remain committed to helping Baltimore City secure needed resources in its effort to fight violent crime and strengthen community policing.
---

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: voting rights under attack
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ For anyone who reads the news, it is increasingly clear that voting rights are under attack across our country.

In the 2016 election alone, fourteen states enacted new voting restrictions for the first time -- raising the burdens on eligible voters and, in many cases, disenfranchising voters altogether.

Under the Trump Administration, Sessions' Justice Department has stopped contesting these and other suppression efforts that are making it more difficult for thousands of Americans to exercise their constitutional right to vote.

And that's only the beginning -- growing threats of hacking and outside interference are threatening to undermine trust in our democratic elections.

[**That 's why I have joined with Senator Cory Booker and others to introduce the Vote by Mail Act to help make our elections more secure and accessible. I hope you will join us to support Vote by Mail and protect our democracy. **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd7/70cdc2ba/469441c7/397df0b/1002617410/VEsH/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/vote-by-mail/van-hollen/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+why+I+have+joined+with+Senato&utm_campaign=em081017&source=em081017")

By requiring physical paper ballots that can't be hacked and can be audited and recounted, Vote by Mail increases the accuracy of our voter rolls and creates a paper trail that allows voters and election officials to track ballots.

By requiring states to ensure that every citizen who provides identifying information to state motor vehicle authorities is automatically registered to vote, this bill also increases turnout, reduces the impact of voter suppression laws and puts an end to long lines on Election Day.

More than anything else, Vote by Mail reinforces the simple democratic truth that elections should be about ADDING votes, not SUBTRACTING votes -- and we need your help to raise awareness.

[**Restoring democracy shouldn 't be a Democratic or Republican issue, it's an American issue. Will you join Senator Booker and myself in support of the Vote by Mail Act now and help strengthen our election system and protect our democracy from attackers, both domestic and foreign? >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd7/70cdc2ba/469441c7/397df08/1002617410/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/vote-by-mail/van-hollen/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Restoring+democracy+shouldnrsquot+be+a+D&utm_campaign=em081017&source=em081017")

Thank you,

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator
